tools.name: taxsort_check_balance
tools.description:
Check a patron's credit balance at this operator.

This is the patron's spending balance — credits purchased via
Lightning for tool calls at this operator. For the operator's
own balance at the Authority (needed to certify patron purchases),
use authority_check_balance instead.

Free — no credits re

tools.name: taxsort_purchase_credits
tools.description:
Buy credits via Bitcoin Lightning.

Creates a Lightning invoice. Pay it with any Lightning wallet,
then call check_payment to confirm. Proof of npub ownership is
required so credits land in the correct ledger.

Free — no credits required to call.

Args:
    npub: The Nostr public key (npub1...) the

tools.name: taxsort_check_payment
tools.description:
Check the payment status of a Lightning invoice.

Call after paying the invoice from purchase_credits.
Free — no credits required. Proof of npub ownership is required
to prevent credit-grant front-running by an observer of the
invoice ID.

Args:
    invoice_id: The invoice ID returned by purchase_cr

tools.name: taxsort_restore_credits
tools.description:
Credit a patron's ledger from a BTCPay-settled invoice.

**RESTRICTED to the operator** — the operator owns the books and is
the only party who can issue a manual credit grant. Patrons who
believe they paid but never got credits must escalate to the
operator's support, who then invokes this tool on

tools.name: taxsort_account_statement
tools.description:
Generate a patron's account statement at this operator.

Returns the patron's purchase history, active credit tranches,
per-tool usage breakdown, and recent daily usage logs. This is
the patron's spending account — not the operator's Authority
tax balance.

Free — no credits consumed. Proof of npub

tools.name: taxsort_account_statement_infographic
tools.description:
Generate a visual SVG infographic of your account statement.

Returns the same data as account_statement, rendered as a dark-themed
SVG graphic with balance hero, metrics cards, health gauge, tranche
table, and tool usage breakdown. Costs 1 api_sat per call. Proof is
verified by ``debit_or_deny`` be

tools.name: taxsort_service_status
tools.description: Check the health and configuration of this service. Free.

tools.name: taxsort_get_operator_onboarding_status
tools.description:
Report this operator's configuration readiness.

Shows which operator settings are configured, which are missing,
and how to deliver each missing value. For patron-level credential
status, use get_patron_onboarding_status instead. Free.

tools.name: taxsort_get_patron_onboarding_status
tools.description:
Report a patron's credential readiness for this operator.

For set-once services (eXcalibur, TheBrain), shows which patron
secrets are configured and which are missing. For dynamic/OAuth2
services (Schwab), reports that no patron credentials are needed.
Free. Proof of npub ownership is required beca

tools.name: taxsort_session_status
tools.description:
Check operator readiness. Returns the operator lifecycle
state and clear guidance on what to do next. Free.

Lifecycle states:
- ready: Operator is warm and fully operational — vault AND pricing
  model verified. Proceed with tool calls.
- warming_up: Operator is initializing (cold start). Try a too

tools.name: taxsort_request_credential_channel
tools.description:
Open a Secure Courier channel for credential delivery.

This is the CREDENTIAL-DELIVERY flow — use it to hand over a service
secret (API keys, tokens). To merely prove you control an npub (the
usual answer to a ``proof_required`` error), use ``request_npub_proof``
instead. Note: dynamic/OAuth2 servi

tools.name: taxsort_receive_credentials
tools.description:
Pick up credentials from the Secure Courier.

Completes the CREDENTIAL-DELIVERY flow (the ownership-proof
counterpart is ``receive_npub_proof``).

**Call this only after the user confirms they have replied.**
Deterministic, one-shot retrieval: name the response you want with
``(sender_npub, service,

tools.name: taxsort_forget_credentials
tools.description:
Delete vaulted credentials for a specific service and npub.

For operator credentials, pass the operator's own npub. For patron
credentials, pass the patron's npub. Always requires proof of
npub ownership — a deletion is as destructive as a write.

Args:
    service: The credential service to forget

tools.name: taxsort_update_patron_credential
tools.description:
Add or update a single patron credential field.

Merges into existing stored credentials without affecting
other fields. Useful for setting an account identifier
after OAuth, changing a default brain, etc. Free. Proof of
npub ownership is required — this is a write to the patron's
sensitive credenti

tools.name: taxsort_delete_patron_credential
tools.description:
Remove a single patron credential field.

Deletes one field from stored credentials without affecting
other fields. Free. Proof of npub ownership is required —
this is a write to the patron's sensitive credential vault.

Args:
    npub: The patron's Nostr public key (npub1...).
    dpop_token: A kin

tools.name: taxsort_get_patron_credential_fields
tools.description:
List stored patron credential field names (not values).

Returns the names of fields stored for a patron. Values
are never exposed — use this to verify which fields are
configured. Free. Proof of npub ownership is required: the
list of configured fields is itself sensitive (reveals
which integration

tools.name: taxsort_request_npub_proof
tools.description:
Request npub ownership proof from a patron via Nostr DM.

This is the npub-OWNERSHIP-PROOF flow — use it when a call returns
``proof_required``. It proves the caller controls an npub; it does
NOT deliver any service secret. To hand an operator its API keys or
OAuth secrets, use ``request_credential_

tools.name: taxsort_receive_npub_proof
tools.description:
Receive npub ownership confirmation from a patron.

Completes the npub-OWNERSHIP-PROOF flow (the credential-delivery
counterpart is ``receive_credentials``).

**Call this only after the user confirms they have replied.**
Deterministic, one-shot retrieval: name the response with
``(patron_npub, dpop_

tools.name: taxsort_check_proof_status
tools.description:
Check whether a previously-cached dpop_token is still valid.

Mirrors ``check_oauth_status`` for the npub-proof flow: a calling
agent can ask "will my next paid call accept this dpop_token?"
before burning credits on a guaranteed failure.

Free, no side effects — does not evict the cache or touch re

tools.name: taxsort_oracle_how_to_join
tools.description: Get DPYC onboarding instructions from the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: taxsort_oracle_get_tax_rate
tools.description: Get the current DPYC certification tax rate. Free.

tools.name: taxsort_oracle_lookup_member
tools.description: Look up a DPYC community member by npub. Free.

tools.name: taxsort_oracle_about
tools.description: Describe the DPYC ecosystem via the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: taxsort_oracle_network_advisory
tools.description: Get active network advisories from the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: taxsort_check_authority_balance
tools.description:
Check this operator's tax balance at the Authority.

Returns the sats available for certifying patron credit purchases.
When this balance reaches zero, patron top-ups cannot be certified
and the operator must call purchase_credits on the Authority.

This is the operator's own funding — not a patron

tools.name: taxsort_get_pricing_model
tools.description:
Get the active pricing model for this operator. Free.

If no model exists, self-initializes a scaffold with all
registered tools at 0 sats.  No economic data from code.

tools.name: taxsort_set_pricing_model
tools.description:
Set the active pricing model. RESTRICTED to operator.

Requires a valid proof (Schnorr-signed kind-27235 event)
proving the caller holds the operator's nsec.

tools.name: taxsort_reset_pricing_model
tools.description:
Erase all pricing models and restore a viable default.

Deletes every stored model, then self-initializes a fresh one
from the tool registry — all tools at 0 sats with proper UUIDs.
Returns the new model.

RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof (nsec-signed).

tools.name: taxsort_request_adoption
tools.description:
Ask a chosen Authority to adopt this operator (deferred courtship).

RESTRICTED to the operator — requires proof the caller controls this
operator's npub. Resolves the Authority's MCP endpoint from the
community registry, mints an inline ownership proof with this
operator's nsec, and delivers the re

tools.name: taxsort_adoption_status
tools.description:
Check this operator's adoption-request status at a chosen Authority.

Free. Polls the Authority MCP-to-MCP for the status of this operator's
request (pending / approved / rejected / provisioned).

tools.name: taxsort_restore_neon_schema
tools.description:
Re-run ``ensure_schema()`` on every NeonVault this operator uses.

Diagnostic / recovery tool for the case where the Neon HTTP SQL API
is returning persistent 4xx errors and the operator suspects the
schema isn't there or grants are wrong. Idempotent — uses
``CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`` so a succes

tools.name: taxsort_list_canonical_identities
tools.description:
Return canonical (tool_id, mcp_name, …) for every registered tool.

The authoritative source for any client (Studio, agents, FE)
that needs to know how this MCP identifies its tools.
Reconcile uses this output to UUID-join against the stored
pricing model — no name-based UUID derivation, no guessing

tools.name: taxsort_get_nostr_profile
tools.description:
Read an npub's public Nostr profile (NIP-01 kind-0 metadata).

Free, no proof — the data is already public on relays. Returns the
latest metadata fields (name, display_name, about, picture, banner,
nip05, website, lud16) or an empty profile if none is published.

tools.name: taxsort_publish_nostr_profile
tools.description:
Publish a CLIENT-SIGNED kind-0 profile to relays for an npub.

The wheel never holds a patron nsec. The frontend signs the kind-0
metadata event with the patron's session key or a NIP-07 extension and
passes the signed event (JSON) here; the wheel verifies the signature
matches the npub, then relays

tools.name: taxsort_check_price
tools.description:
Preview the effective cost of a tool call.

Shows the base cost and any constraint effects (discounts, free
trials, surge pricing). Free — no credits required.

Args:
    tool_id: Either the tool's UUID (from the pricing model) or a
        bare capability string (e.g. ``"deal_scenario"``). FE

tools.name: taxsort_list_constraint_types
tools.description:
List all available constraint types and their parameter schemas.

Returns the type, category, description, and parameter specs for
every constraint that can be used in a pricing pipeline.
Free — no credits required.

tools.name: taxsort_mint_coupon
tools.description:
Create a new operator-owned discount coupon.

Args:
    name: The catchy code patrons type to redeem
        (operator-scoped uniqueness).
    discount_percent: Percentage off the base price (0-100).
    valid_from: ISO-8601 datetime when the coupon becomes active.
    valid_until: ISO-8601 datetime

tools.name: taxsort_list_coupons
tools.description:
List every coupon this operator has minted (newest first).

Each row carries the current ``times_redeemed`` counter — the
Studio renders a progress bar from this against ``total_uses``.
RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof.

tools.name: taxsort_update_coupon
tools.description:
Patch a coupon's editable fields.

Pass only the fields you want to change.  To set a cap to
unlimited (NULL in the schema), pass ``clear_uses_per_patron=true``
or ``clear_total_uses=true``.  Renaming the code is allowed —
existing patron redemption rows survive (they key on coupon id).

RESTRICTED

tools.name: taxsort_delete_coupon
tools.description:
Delete a coupon.  Cascades to all patron redemptions.

Any chain step referencing the deleted coupon_id becomes a
no-op (the constraint returns neutral on unknown ids) — the
Studio surfaces orphan references as warnings.

RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof.

tools.name: taxsort_redeem_coupon
tools.description:
Claim a coupon by its name (the code the operator shared).

Looks up the operator's coupon by ``code``, validates the window
and total cap, and records a per-patron redemption row.
Subsequent paid tool calls on this MCP auto-apply the discount
until ``uses_per_patron`` is exhausted.

Free — no credi

tools.name: taxsort_list_my_coupons
tools.description:
List the coupons this patron has redeemed on this operator.

Returns both active and exhausted redemptions with a per-row
``status`` (``active`` / ``window_closed`` / ``patron_limit`` /
``total_limit``).  Free — requires proof of ``npub``.

tools.name: taxsort_forget_coupon
tools.description:
Remove a coupon from this patron's redemption list.

Cosmetic only — the coupon itself still exists at the operator,
and the patron can re-redeem the same code later while the
window allows.  Free — requires proof of ``npub``.

tools.name: taxsort_notarize_ledger
tools.description:
Build a Merkle tree of all patron balances and submit the root
to Bitcoin via OpenTimestamps.

Operator-only background task. Bitcoin confirmation takes 1-6 hours.
Free — no credits required.

tools.name: taxsort_get_notarization_proof
tools.description:
Generate a Merkle inclusion proof that a patron's balance was
included in a Bitcoin-notarized snapshot.

Args:
    notarization_id: The notarization record ID.
    npub: The patron's Nostr public key (npub1...).

tools.name: taxsort_list_notarizations
tools.description:
List recent Bitcoin notarization records.

Args:
    limit: Maximum records to return (default 20).
    status: Optional filter (e.g., 'submitted', 'confirmed').

tools.name: taxsort_verify_passphrase
tools.description: Verify a passphrase to unlock a timed-out session.

tools.name: taxsort_create_session
tools.description: Create a new TaxSort session for a tax year.

tools.name: taxsort_get_session
tools.description: Get session details and summary stats.

tools.name: taxsort_list_sessions
tools.description: List all sessions owned by the current patron.

tools.name: taxsort_import_csv
tools.description: Import a CSV file into a session. Content is the raw CSV text. Optional account_name overrides the filename-derived account.

tools.name: taxsort_get_import_stats
tools.description: Get import statistics for a session.

tools.name: taxsort_get_transactions
tools.description:
Get transactions for a session with optional filters.

Returns raw transactions LEFT JOINed with their classifications.
Use unclassified_only=true to fetch pages of transactions needing
classification by the FE. Use date_from/date_to for date range queries.

tools.name: taxsort_get_transactions_paged
tools.description:
Server-side filtered, grouped, sorted, paginated transactions.

group_sort controls the order of groups (A-Z vs Z-A).
sort_col + sort_dir control row order within each group.
When group_by='none', only sort_col + sort_dir apply.

tools.name: taxsort_save_classifications
tools.description:
Bulk write classifications from the FE.

classifications is a JSON array of objects, each with:
  - id: raw_transaction_id
  - category, subcategory (required)
  - confidence, reason, merchant, description_override (optional)
  - classified_by: 'ai' | 'rule' | 'manual' (default 'ai')

tools.name: taxsort_delete_classification
tools.description: Remove a classification, reverting the transaction to unclassified.

tools.name: taxsort_clear_transactions
tools.description: Delete all transactions and classifications for a session, so CSVs can be re-imported.

tools.name: taxsort_delete_account_transactions
tools.description: Delete all transactions and classifications for a specific imported account.

tools.name: taxsort_reset_classifications
tools.description: Delete all classifications but keep the imported transactions.

tools.name: taxsort_get_amount_neighbors
tools.description: Fetch transactions with the same amount within ±days of a date. Used by the classifier to detect duplicates from overlapping CSV imports.

tools.name: taxsort_get_accounts
tools.description: List all accounts in this session with their types and transaction counts.

tools.name: taxsort_set_account_type
tools.description: Set an account's type: bank (checking/savings), card (credit/debit), investment, or loan.

tools.name: taxsort_get_summary
tools.description: Get a grouped spending summary for tax reporting.

tools.name: taxsort_get_rules
tools.description: Get all classification rules for the current patron.

tools.name: taxsort_save_rule
tools.description:
Create a classification rule.

Provide description_pattern (regex matched case-insensitively against
the transaction description), category, and subcategory. Optionally add
amount_operator (lt, lte, gt, gte, eq, neq) and amount_value to filter
by amount. When the compound constraint matches, categor

tools.name: taxsort_delete_rule
tools.description: Delete a classification rule by ID.

tools.name: taxsort_apply_rules
tools.description: Apply rules to unclassified transactions in a session.

tools.name: taxsort_count_rule_matches
tools.description: Count how many transactions match a rule pattern (live preview).

tools.name: taxsort_get_custom_categories
tools.description: Get custom categories defined by this user.

tools.name: taxsort_save_custom_category
tools.description: Add a custom category/subcategory (e.g. Personal / Auto Gas).

tools.name: taxsort_delete_custom_category
tools.description: Delete a custom category.

tools.name: taxsort_create_share_token
tools.description: Create a share token so another user can access this session.

tools.name: taxsort_load_share_token
tools.description: Load a shared session via a share token.

tools.name: taxsort_get_github_token
tools.description: Get the GitHub token for creating issues in the taxsort-mcp repo.

tools.name: taxsort_get_anthropic_key
tools.description: Get the Anthropic API key for FE-driven classification.

tools.name: taxsort_report_api_usage
tools.description: Report Anthropic API usage from FE classification for cost tracking.

tools.name: taxsort_get_api_usage_stats
tools.description: Get aggregated API usage statistics for cost analysis.

tools.name: taxsort_create_feedback_issue
tools.description: Create a GitHub issue for bug reports, feature requests, or feedback.

tools.name: taxsort_list_feedback_issues
tools.description: List feedback issues submitted by this patron.

tools.name: taxsort_session_heartbeat
tools.description: Presence heartbeat. Returns who else is active in this session.

tools.name: taxsort_ask_advisor
tools.description: Ask the Financial Advisor about using TaxSort.

tools.name: taxsort_ask_tax_researcher
tools.description: Ask the Tax Code Researcher about IRS provisions.

tools.name: taxsort_request_unlock
tools.description: Request a session unlock after timeout.

tools.name: taxsort_check_unlock
tools.description: Check if the unlock response is valid.

findings.detail: Description of "taxsort_restore_credits" is 935 chars, far longer than this server's median (165). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: taxsort_session_status, taxsort_request_credential_channel, taxsort_receive_credentials, taxsort_request_npub_proof, taxsort_receive_npub_proof, taxsort_get_github_token, taxsort_list_feedback_issues · Private data: taxsort_purchase_credits, taxsort_restore_credits, taxsort_get_operator_onboarding_status, taxsort_get_patron_onboarding_status, taxsort_session_status, taxsort_request_credential_channel, taxsort_receive_credentials, taxsort_forget_credentials, taxsort_update_patron_credential, taxsort_delete_patron_credential, taxsort_get_patron_credential_fields, taxsort_request_npub_proof, taxsort_receive_npub_proof, taxsort_request_adoption, taxsort_publish_nostr_profile, taxsort_mint_coupon, taxsort_redeem_coupon, taxsort_get_notarization_proof, taxsort_list_notarizations, taxsort_import_csv, taxsort_create_share_token, taxsort_load_share_token, taxsort_get_github_token, taxsort_get_anthropic_key · Public sink: taxsort_request_credential_channel, taxsort_publish_nostr_profile, taxsort_notarize_ledger, taxsort_create_share_token, taxsort_load_share_token · Destructive: taxsort_forget_credentials, taxsort_delete_patron_credential, taxsort_reset_pricing_model, taxsort_restore_neon_schema, taxsort_delete_coupon, taxsort_forget_coupon, taxsort_delete_classification, taxsort_clear_transactions, taxsort_delete_account_transactions, taxsort_reset_classifications, taxsort_delete_rule, taxsort_delete_custom_category.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: taxsort_session_status, taxsort_request_credential_channel, taxsort_receive_credentials, taxsort_request_npub_proof, taxsort_receive_npub_proof, taxsort_get_github_token, taxsort_list_feedback_issues · Private data: taxsort_purchase_credits, taxsort_restore_credits, taxsort_get_operator_onboarding_status, taxsort_get_patron_onboarding_status, taxsort_session_status, taxsort_request_credential_channel, taxsort_receive_credentials, taxsort_forget_credentials, taxsort_update_patron_credential, taxsort_delete_patron_credential, taxsort_get_patron_credential_fields, taxsort_request_npub_proof, taxsort_receive_npub_proof, taxsort_request_adoption, taxsort_publish_nostr_profile, taxsort_mint_coupon, taxsort_redeem_coupon, taxsort_get_notarization_proof, taxsort_list_notarizations, taxsort_import_csv, taxsort_create_share_token, taxsort_load_share_token, taxsort_get_github_token, taxsort_get_anthropic_key · Public sink: taxsort_request_credential_channel, taxsort_publish_nostr_profile, taxsort_notarize_ledger, taxsort_create_share_token, taxsort_load_share_token · Destructive: taxsort_forget_credentials, taxsort_delete_patron_credential, taxsort_reset_pricing_model, taxsort_restore_neon_schema, taxsort_delete_coupon, taxsort_forget_coupon, taxsort_delete_classification, taxsort_clear_transactions, taxsort_delete_account_transactions, taxsort_reset_classifications, taxsort_delete_rule, taxsort_delete_custom_category

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

remediation.title: Tool description changed since last scan
remediation.detail: Tool "taxsort_session_status" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet:
before: "Check operator readiness. Returns the operator lifecycle
state and clear guidance on what to do next. Free.

Lifecycle states:
- ready: Operator is warm and fully operational — vault AND pricing
  model verified. Proceed with tool calls.
- warming_up: Operator is initializing (cold start). Try a too" → after: "Check operator readiness. Returns the operator lifecycle
state and clear guidance on what to do next. Free.

Lifecycle states:
- ready: Operator is warm and fully operational — vault AND pricing
  model verified. Proceed with tool calls.
- warming_up: Operator is initializing (cold start). Try a too"

remediation.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
